intent: Write a deep research brief on 'starting a home fitness routine for under $200' that triangulates THREE sources. (1) One Stop Market shopping site — browse sports-outdoors and health-household categories and collect at least 5 products (e.g. resistance bands, yoga mat, dumbbell, foam roller, jump rope). Record name, price, rating, and review count from the detail pages. (2) Reddit — browse /f/LifeProTips AND /f/personalfinance for advice on home workouts, avoiding gym contracts, or which cheap equipment actually gets used. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, and comment count. (3) Wikipedia — open the articles on 'Physical exercise' and 'Strength training' (or one similar) and extract 2-3 factual claims about minimum recommended weekly activity or beginner-appropriate movements. The report must: (a) propose a $200 starter kit from the storefront with itemized prices and a running total, (b) justify each item with either a Reddit testimonial or a Wikipedia-grounded exercise rationale, (c) flag any storefront item the community considers useless, (d) conclude with a one-week sample plan referencing the Wikipedia exercise guidance. Cite every data point with a markdown link.
author_notes: Three-site hard task: storefront products + community advice + encyclopedic grounding for exercise claims. Tests full cross-source synthesis.
